fields.title: My first post!
fields.author: Jarrod Wandrey
fields.content:
Hello everyone! 

This is the first blog post I am creating after finishing up the foundation of this app. Currently the app has basic CRUD functionality and that is about it, although there is more I would like to add as things go forward. I would like to use this to track my progress as I get back into the groove of hobby coding with hopes to improve my skills and becoming a better dev.

I have plans for more updates to this app which will be my main focus and what most of these posts will be about for the time being. I have already learned a lot when it comes to Django, in terms of creating services/models/views. Its exciting to try something new and struggle a little to figure something out. 

More posts to come :)

fields.title: The next hurdle
fields.author: Jarrod Wandrey
fields.content:
Hi again,

The next step in this project is going to be users and auth, which I have never really done before so i'm hoping this goes well! Since the base page for now is basically done its time to start polishing up some things and having this work as intended.

The plans for this new Accounts app is to have some sort of auth to make sure users can and cannot do certain things. A few things that come to mind are:
- No users except for me should be allowed to create, edit or delete blog posts (those are mine)
- All users that are not me should be able to see all posts as well as hopefully comment or heart or interact in some way with them.

This will allow me to get a better understanding of the logic behind user registration and authentication, which I am excited to learn.

fields.title: The basis of registration is complete
fields.author: Jarrod Wandrey
fields.content:
Progress was made! 

User registration and login/logout seem to be working correctly and implemented, it was very helpful that Django has a lot of built in functionality for auth and users. This made my life really easy. I referenced this page for some help regarding registration and to get that sorted, it is a good resource: https://django-learning.hashnode.dev/how-to-create-a-user-registration-page-in-django

Next I need to get permission sorted and hopefully some QOL things regarding the user and the page actions. I learned a good bit about views and linking two apps together under one project, its so interesting how much is happening under the hood with Django. I also got some exposure to forms, which I think are the staple when creating things like users.

fields.title: New week, new hurdles
fields.author: JarrodWandrey
fields.content:
Hi all,

This is week two of these semi-daily posts I am attempting to write to track my dev progress. I have a few ideas this week for this blog page that I am going to attempt to conquer. First, I want to add a comment system for these post that any user can send. This will allow me to work with foreign keys in Django and add another layer of interact-ability to the page. Second, I think adding some more customization to the other users who visit this page would be nice. Maybe a upvote system along with the comments to make posts more intractable?

I have already gone ahead and added the model for comments, as well as another small QOL change to autofill the Author input box when creating a post so I dont have to type that in every time. I need to now create the view and the proper html to add said comment/upvote.

fields.title: Rough draft for comments is mostly done!
fields.author: JarrodWandrey
fields.content:
The comment functionality seems to be working which is nice, although there are some small things visually I want to adjust down the line. All users are now allowed to add a comment to any post in the list, the author information is automatically filled as your username on the site as a QOL change. The formatting is a little odd since I opted to use a textarea for the comments as well to preserve formatting, but that is something I'll look at down the road. 

Some new challenges that I have through about that could be good for future development:
- Figuring out the best way to handle this database and persist the data, currently its committed to the repo but that is kinda a bad way to do that 
- Getting this app to a deployed state so I can learn how to do all of that
- Small feature for like 'upvoting' posts, and possibly sorting on the home page

fields.title: Adding Comment and Likes to posts!
fields.author: JarrodWandrey
fields.content:
Another Day!

Now that the blog is in a working state, I am just adding small things to polish everything up and make it more user friendly. Today I handled adding a comment and like count on the home page as well as adding a int for 'likes' to the post model. That seems to be working properly and was a nice small change to add a lot of character to the page and have a little more interaction with users. 

My next BIG plan is to host my database through AWS RDS, that way I can stop committing database changes to my github repo like a bot... That will be a larger hurdle as I have to jump into the world of AWS and learn how to host that and connect it to the Django project. It is exciting though because if I can get that working properly than I can even look at hosting this app on a EC2 instance as well!

fields.title: Its almost the new year!
fields.author: JarrodWandrey
fields.content:
Happy Holidays,

Its been a couple weeks since I have last look at this app, granted most of the functionality is done at this point. I have been busy over the holidays with life/work so I havent been able to commit much to this. I still have plans to update this going forward, those plans include:

- Update like system to only allow one like/user on a single post
- Enhance error handing to be more user friendly
- Get this app deployed somewhere, like AWS or something
- Migrate the database to there so I do not have to commit db changes...

I think after these changes this app can be considered finished! Then I can transition to the next project, whatever that might be.

fields.title: New years update!
fields.author: JarrodWandrey
fields.content:
Happy *belated new year,

I am finally back after the holidays and the new year. I have made a small update today to improve the QOL of users on the site. I had introduced a new table to now track who is liking which post, this allows me to keep a current and accurate count of likes on a post and avoid duplicate likes/user. 

At this point all that is left for this is to deploy it and create a standalone DB like I had mentioned in my last post. This I imagine will be tougher because I have never had to do anything like this before, but I am excited to learn and get something tangible.

fields.author: JarrodWandrey
fields.content: Next issue now is tracking who likes what so one person cant like a post 100x!
